X - RAY (KUB)

• Spine shadow appears normal.

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an X-ray KUB (Kidney, Ureter, Bladder) performed on a 36-year-old male. The report notes that a previously identified calculus in the right upper ureter is now clear and a DJ stent is visible. The left renal region shows no radio-opacities, bowel gas shadows are increased, and the ileo-psoas and spine shadows are normal. The report is signed by a Consultant Radiologist and includes a disclaimer that it is not a final diagnosis and not valid for medico-legal purposes.
key_observations: Patient is a 36-year-old male.
DJ stent is seen in situ in the right renal/ureteric region.
Previously mentioned calculus in the right upper ureter appears clear.
No obvious radio-opacity seen in the left renal region.
Bowel gas shadows are increased.
Ileo-psoas and spine shadows are normal.
Report is signed by a Consultant Radiologist with a disclaimer.
Date of report is 06/04/2026.

records.stage_evidence: No contrast media or catheter tip visible in urinary tract; no devices in field
records.summary: A posteroanterior (PA) abdominal X-ray (KUB) showing the pelvicalyceal system with a visible urinary catheter in situ. The patient is 36 years old, male. The image was taken on April 6, 2026.
key_observations: A urinary catheter is visible, extending from the bladder to the external urethral meatus.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visualized.
Patient age and gender: 36Y.M (36-year-old male).
Date of imaging: 06-04-26 (April 6, 2026).

records.stage_evidence: Clinical report of CT scan findings

ocr.verbatim:
RADIOLOGY DEPARTMENT
NAME. 
REF BY 
DATE; 02/04/2026
AGE./SEX 36Y/M
PATIENT NO. 8

CT KUB (PLAIN)
Sequential axial CT images of abdomen involving KUB region were obtained.
Right kidney: 10.6x5.6cm in size. 4mm calculus seen at lower pole.
There is 8mm calculus (800HU) in right upper ureter causing mild hydro-uretronephrosis.
Left kidney: 9.2x5.2cm in size. It appears normal in size, shape & attenuation. Left ureter appears non dilated.
Urinary bladder appears normal.
Visualised small & large bowel loops show normal distention & attenuation.
Pelvic viscera appear normal.
No free / loculated fluid collection is seen. No evident abdominal lymphadenopathy is seen.
Bone window images show no gross abnormality.
IMPRESSION:
Right upper uretric calculus & Non obstructing renal calculus.

MBBS, MD,
Consultant Radiologist

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a CT KUB (Plain) scan performed on a 36-year-old male patient on April 2, 2026. The report details findings of a 4mm calculus in the right kidney and an 8mm calculus in the right upper ureter causing mild hydronephrosis. The left kidney and other abdominal structures appear normal. The impression is right upper ureteric calculus and a non-obstructing renal calculus.
key_observations: CT KUB (Plain) scan performed on April 2, 2026
Right kidney: 4mm calculus at lower pole
Right upper ureter: 8mm calculus (800HU) causing mild hydronephrosis
Left kidney and ureter appear normal
Urinary bladder, bowel loops, pelvic viscera, and bones appear normal
Final impression: Right upper ureteric calculus and non-obstructing renal calculus

records.stage_evidence: No contrast media or catheter visible; diagnostic KUB view
records.summary: A posteroanterior (PA) abdominal X-ray (KUB) showing the lumbar spine, pelvis, and bowel gas patterns. The patient is a 37-year-old female. The image is dated March 30, 2026.
key_observations: PA view of the abdomen and pelvis
